repos.name: YeerooXY/shooter-mover
repos.purpose: Single product repository containing the Unity project, engine-independent domain core, Unity adapters, modular content packages, source-asset manifests, verification tooling, lifecycle artifacts, and review evidence.
contains: Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/Domain/ — engine-independent authoritative rules and state
Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/Application/ — use-case orchestration and local service interfaces
Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/UnityAdapters/ — Input System, 2D physics, scenes, rendering, audio, and platform adapters
Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/Presentation/ and Assets/ShooterMover/UI/ — HUD and menu flows
Assets/ShooterMover/Content/ — shared typed definitions and reusable modules
Assets/ShooterMover/ContentPackages/ — independently owned weapons, enemies, rooms, encounters, and environment packages
Assets/ShooterMover/Generated/ — rebuilt registries and deterministic review snapshots; never hand edited
Assets/ShooterMover/Tests/ and Assets/ShooterMover/TestSupport/ — edit-mode, play-mode, performance, recovery, and build smoke coverage
Packages/ and ProjectSettings/ — pinned Unity LTS project baseline and locked packages
tools/ and .github/workflows/ — deterministic validation, generation, build, and artifact tooling
docs/ — architecture decisions, verification plans, toolchain lock, art pipeline, provenance, debt register, and operational policies
source-assets/ — lightweight manifests, export recipes, references, and Git-LFS-tracked production source files approved for repository storage
assembly/ — requirements, planning package, later task state, handoffs, and review traces
depends_on: Pinned Unity LTS editor and package lock
Approved offline source-art tools recorded in docs/toolchain/TOOLCHAIN_LOCK.md
Git LFS only for approved large repository assets; separate encrypted backup for full source-art history
GitHub pull requests and layered validation
excludes: Separate backend, database, account, analytics, cloud-save, storefront, networking, relay, or mobile repositories during the internal MVP
Canonical task backlog, task batches, claims, or implementation run reports in the planning PR
Generated build artifacts, Library/, Temp/, Logs/, Obj/, local caches, credentials, signing keys, and private service secrets
Unlicensed, provenance-unknown, or release-bound source assets without recorded approval
Future Android, co-op, additional factions, full campaign, and mature economy implementation
